data.title: T'way Air offers limited-time deals for flights from Europe to Seoul, valid until July 31, 2025. #KoreaTrip #TwayAir
data.description: Travelers can now take advantage of limited-time offers to fly from major European cities such as Rome, Paris, Barcelona, Frankfurt, and Zagreb to Seoul. T'way Air, South Korea's top low-cost carrier, unveiled its latest promotion on July 18, 2025, available exclusively on its website. This promotion is in effect until July 31st and covers flights from all five European cities to Seoul.

data.title: vivo wins "Innovate for Impact" award at ITU AI for Good Global Summit for empowering accessibility.
data.description: In an event held in SHENZHEN, China on July 18, 2025, vivo was awarded the "Innovate for Impact" best use case accolade in the accessibility category at the AI for Good Global Summit. This recognition underscores vivo's dedication to enhancing accessibility through on-device intelligence. The award signifies vivo's commitment to innovation and positive impact, showcasing their efforts to empower individuals with diverse needs.

data.title: Charles Schwab will release Q2 earnings with analysts predicting $1.07 EPS, up from 73 cents last year, and $5.64 billion revenue, up from $4.69 billion last year. Stock closed at $93.10, gained 2%.
data.description: Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE: SCHW) is set to unveil its second-quarter earnings on July 18 before the market opens. Analysts are predicting earnings of $1.07 per share, a significant increase from the 73 cents per share reported in the same period last year. The Texas-headquartered company is expected to announce quarterly revenue of $5.64 billion, up from $4.69 billion a year ago. In the first quarter, Charles Schwab exceeded expectations with its financial performance. As a result, the company's shares rose by 2% to $93.10 at the closing of Thursday's trading session. For more in-depth analysis and up-to-date analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga Pro.

data.title: Construction of first homes in phase II of Luuslangi project to start on 1 August 2025 by Mitt & Perlebach OÜ.
data.description: Liven Kodu 12 OÜ, a subsidiary of Liven AS working on the Luuslang project, has penned a deal with Mitt & Perlebach OÜ as the general contractor for phase II's initial residential buildings. The construction of these four-story buildings located at Jalami 6/1 and Jalami 6/2 in Haabersti's Astangu area is set to commence on August 1, 2025. Mitt & Perlebach OÜ was also responsible for phase I's construction activities.

data.title: Top Wall Street forecasters are revising American Express expectations. Q2 earnings expected at $3.86 per share, $17.7B revenue. Stock closed at $315.35.
data.description: American Express Company (NYSE: AXP) is set to unveil its second-quarter earnings before market opening on Friday, July 18. Analysts predict earnings per share to be $3.86, compared to $4.15 in the same quarter last year. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $17.7 billion, up from $16.33 billion the previous year. On June 17, the board announced a quarterly dividend of 82 cents per common share. Closing at $315.35 on Thursday, American Express shares saw a 1.1% increase. For more information, including the latest analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
